heading: Maine
label: 99 Maine
text: Maine constitutes one judicial district.Court shall be held at Bangor and Portland.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 160 (, ; , ; , §§ 1, 3, ; , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 8536 Stat. 1114Dec. 22, 1911, ch. 737 Stat. 51Sept. 8, 1916, ch. 47539 Stat. 850Mar. 4, 1923, ch. 27942 Stat. 1506Changes in arrangement and phraseology were made.

Amendments1978— struck out provision for two separate divisions, (1) the Northern Division comprising the counties of Aroostook, Hancock, Penobscot, Piscataquis, Somerset, Waldo, and Washington and (2) the Southern Division comprising the counties of Androscoggin, Cumberland, Franklin, Kennebec, Knox, Lincoln, Oxford, Sagadahoc, and York.Pub. L. 95–573
Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentAmendment by effective 180 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 95–573section 6 of Pub. L. 95–573section 93 of this titleNov. 2, 1978
